title: The Fox and the Stork
lines: A Fox once invited a Stork to dinner.
He set before her a large flat plate of soup.
The Stork, whose bill was long and narrow, could not eat the soup, while the Fox, with his short snout, lapped it up readily.
"I hope you are enjoying your dinner," said the Fox with a sly smile.
The Stork said nothing, but accepted the Fox's invitation to dine with her the next evening.
She served the dinner in a tall, narrow-necked jar.
The Fox could not insert his snout into the jar, while the Stork, with her long bill, ate her fill.
"I believe you know the saying, 'One good turn deserves another.'"
And the Stork flew away, leaving the Fox hungry and thoughtful.
moral: Do unto others as you would have them do unto you.
